title: Agentic Finance Platform — System Design
description: Design the platform that lets finance agents do real accounting work — AP, AR, reconciliation, close — where the action behind the gate is irreversible: money moves and journals post. It builds on Agentic Platform Foundations (identity/delegation, guardrails, durable execution) but inverts the priorities of the Developer vertical: correctness and auditability over availability. The agent runs as a deterministic pipeline (extract → match → propose), never freely mutating the ledger; it proposes journals that a separate checker approves (maker-checker, segregation of duties enforced architecturally); every posting is idempotent; and the audit artifact is an immutable decision record, not a chain-of-thought dump.

nodes.label: Maker (Analyst)
nodes.description: Finance analyst who initiates work; the agent acts as an automated maker that proposes.

nodes.label: Checker (Approver)
nodes.description: Separate approver who reviews and authorizes money movement / journal posting.

nodes.label: Source Documents
nodes.description: Invoices, bank feeds, POs, contracts arriving by email, portal, EDI, or PDF.

nodes.label: Finance API
nodes.description: Authenticates inbound docs and routes to the deterministic pipeline.

nodes.label: Deterministic Pipeline
nodes.description: Authored DAG (extract -> match -> propose -> maker/checker), not an autonomous loop. The control plane.

nodes.label: Extract / Code Agent
nodes.description: LLM inside a bounded node: extracts invoice fields and proposes a GL coding.

nodes.label: Deterministic Match Engine
nodes.description: Rule-based three-way / PO match and threshold checks — deterministic, not the model.

nodes.label: Inference Backend
nodes.description: Shared LLM serving tier (from the Foundations platform).

nodes.label: General Ledger
nodes.description: Authoritative double-entry GL — ground truth. Agents propose journals; only an approved, idempotent post mutates it.

nodes.label: Sub-ledgers & Bank Feeds
nodes.description: AP/AR sub-ledgers, bank statement feeds, and ERP records the agent reconciles against.

nodes.label: Proposed Journals
nodes.description: Pending journal entries the agent has proposed, awaiting checker approval (never posted yet).

nodes.label: Payment Rail
nodes.description: ACH / wire / RTP rails that move money once a payment is approved.

nodes.label: Policy / Controls Engine
nodes.description: Deterministic SOX/ICFR controls: segregation of duties, thresholds, fraud checks, prompt-injection screening of documents.

nodes.label: Identity & Token Broker
nodes.description: Mints scoped, delegated authority for postings/payments; enforces maker != checker.

nodes.label: Immutable Decision Record
nodes.description: Append-only audit record: retrieved records + versions, tool calls, calculations, policy checks, approvals, event IDs.

nodes.label: Work Queue
nodes.description: Levels document intake and carries close/reconciliation batches.

nodes.label: Observability & Eval
nodes.description: Trajectories, exception rates, and eval against ground-truth bookings.

functional: Ingest source documents (invoices, bank feeds, POs, contracts) from email, portal, EDI, and PDF.
Extract and code transactions, then match them deterministically (three-way / PO match, reconciliation).
Propose journal entries and payments — never post or pay autonomously.
Route proposals through maker-checker approval before any ledger mutation or money movement.
Produce an immutable, auditable decision record for every action.
nonFunctional: Correctness and auditability take priority over availability (it is better to halt than to post a wrong entry).
The general ledger is ground truth; agents propose, only an approved idempotent post mutates it.
Segregation of duties: the maker (or agent) can never also be the checker — enforced architecturally.
Every posting and payment is idempotent (exactly-once under retries and crashes).
Continuous SOX/ICFR controls; the audit trail is a first-class artifact.
Deterministic control flow: a known pipeline with LLM calls inside bounded nodes, not an autonomous loop.

capacity.label: Posting semantics
capacity.note: Every journal post / payment carries an idempotency key; a retry never double-posts.

capacity.label: Autonomy
capacity.note: Agents write to a proposals store; only an approved, controlled path mutates the GL.

capacity.label: Failure posture
capacity.note: On ambiguity or control failure, escalate to a human — correctness beats throughput.

api.description: The pipeline submits a proposed journal entry (extracted, coded, matched) for approval — it does not post.
A checker (distinct from the maker) approves; only then does an idempotent post / payment occur.
Fetch the immutable decision record for audit: evidence, calculations, controls, approvals, event IDs.

dataModel.name: proposals
dataModel.note: A proposed journal awaiting approval — never the ledger itself.

dataModel.name: journal_lines
dataModel.note: Double-entry lines of a proposal; debits must equal credits.

dataModel.name: decision_record
dataModel.note: Append-only audit artifact (NOT raw model reasoning).

patterns.name: Workflow vs agent (deterministic pipeline vs probabilistic loop)
patterns.what: An authored DAG (extract -> match -> propose) with LLM calls in bounded nodes, not an autonomous loop.
patterns.whenToUse: When the path is known and correctness/audit dominate.

patterns.name: Human-in-the-loop approval (gate)
patterns.what: A separate checker approves before any ledger mutation or money movement.
patterns.whenToUse: Before every irreversible posting or payment.

patterns.name: Bounded autonomy (agent + deterministic gate)
patterns.what: Agents propose freely but a deterministic maker-checker gate authorizes the irreversible action.
patterns.whenToUse: Propose-only autonomy with a hard approval boundary.

patterns.name: Durable execution / checkpointing (resume after crash)
patterns.what: Idempotent, exactly-once posting so a crash or retry never double-posts.
patterns.whenToUse: Any money movement or ledger write.

patterns.name: Delegation not impersonation (OAuth token exchange, act claim)
patterns.what: Scoped, delegated authority for postings/payments with the agent's own attributable principal.

patterns.name: Prompt-injection defense (CaMeL / Dual-LLM / Plan-then-Execute)
patterns.what: Treat invoices, emails, and documents as untrusted; partition them from posting authority.

patterns.name: LLM-as-judge / trajectory evaluation
patterns.what: Evaluate extractions/codings against ground-truth bookings, and track exception and accuracy rates.

patterns.name: Queue-based load leveling; admission control
patterns.what: Level document intake and close/reconciliation batches.

steps.title: 1. Naive: An Agent That Posts to the Ledger (the baseline)
description: Start with the obvious thing: an autonomous agent reads an invoice, decides the entry, and writes it straight to the general ledger — and pays it.

In finance this is the dangerous version, not the convenient one. The action is irreversible (money moved, the books changed), there is no second pair of eyes, no idempotency (a retry double-pays), and no audit artifact beyond the agent's say-so. Regulators and a CFO who is personally liable will not accept 'the model decided'.
The rest of the interview turns this into a propose-only, maker-checker, deterministic pipeline.

view.caption: An autonomous agent posts straight to the GL and pays — the dangerous baseline finance won't accept.

steps.decisionPrompt: Why is 'the agent writes the journal and pays' unacceptable in finance even if its accuracy is high?
patterns: Workflow vs agent (deterministic pipeline vs probabilistic loop)
whyNow: The naive direct-post makes the irreversibility and audit requirements concrete.

recap.after: An agent can book and pay an invoice end to end.
recap.newRisk: Irreversible, unreviewed, non-idempotent, and unauditable — every finance control is missing.

traps.trap: Letting the agent mutate the ledger directly because its extraction accuracy is high.
traps.why: Accuracy doesn't address irreversibility, segregation of duties, idempotency, or auditability.
traps.instead: Make the agent propose; a separate, controlled path posts.

steps.title: 2. The Ledger Is Ground Truth: Propose, Don't Mutate
description: The grounding corpus for this vertical is the books themselves: the double-entry general ledger, sub-ledgers, and bank feeds. The ledger is ground truth, and only an approved, controlled, idempotent post may change it.

So the agent's output is a proposed journal written to a separate proposals store — debits equal credits, attached to evidence — not a direct GL mutation. This single inversion (propose, don't mutate) is what makes everything else auditable.

view.caption: Agents read the ledger/sub-ledgers as ground truth and write proposed journals to a separate store — never the GL directly.

steps.decisionPrompt: Where does an agent's output go if it must never directly change the books?

concepts.definition: The double-entry GL is the authoritative record; agents read it to reconcile and propose entries, but cannot freely write it.
concepts.whyItMatters: It bounds the agent to a reversible artifact (a proposal) and keeps the irreversible system of record under control.
concepts.example: The agent proposes a journal; the GL is untouched until a checker approves an idempotent post.

whyNow: Establishing propose-only grounding is the foundation the gate and audit record build on.

recap.before: The agent could write the GL.
recap.after: The agent proposes journals against ground truth; the GL is controlled.
recap.newRisk: How is the proposal produced reliably and auditably — by a loop, or a known pipeline?

steps.title: 3. A Deterministic Pipeline, Not a Loop
description: Because the path is known and correctness/audit dominate, the control plane is a deterministic pipeline — extract → match → propose — with LLM calls confined to bounded nodes, rather than an autonomous ReAct loop fenced by a late gate (the §2 selection rule).

The model extracts and codes inside one node; a deterministic match engine does the three-way / PO match and threshold checks; the pipeline writes the proposal. Each node's behavior is testable and its output is logged.

view.caption: An authored DAG: the model extracts/codes in a bounded node; a deterministic engine matches; the pipeline writes a proposal.

steps.decisionPrompt: Why a fixed pipeline here instead of an autonomous agent that figures out the steps?
patterns: Workflow vs agent (deterministic pipeline vs probabilistic loop)

concepts.definition: The model is used for one well-scoped sub-task (field extraction, coding) inside an authored step, not to choose the overall path.
concepts.whyItMatters: It contains non-determinism to where it's needed and keeps the workflow predictable and testable.
concepts.example: The extract node returns structured fields; the deterministic match node decides matched-vs-exception.

whyNow: A known, auditable path is itself a control — the pipeline shape is the right default before adding the gate.

options.name: Deterministic pipeline (recommended)
pros: Every step is logged and auditable.
Non-determinism is confined to bounded nodes.
cons: Less flexible for genuinely novel cases.
Exceptions must route to humans explicitly.

options.name: Autonomous loop + late gate
cons: Non-deterministic, hard to audit, can loop and burn cost.

recap.before: Proposals could come from an opaque loop.
recap.after: A deterministic, testable pipeline produces each proposal.
recap.newRisk: A proposal still must be authorized by someone who isn't the proposer before it posts.

steps.title: 4. The Gate: Maker-Checker & Segregation of Duties
description: This is the crux. The agent is the maker: it proposes. A separate checker — a human, or for low-risk items a deterministic threshold — authorizes the post or payment. The maker can never also be the checker, and that segregation of duties is enforced architecturally by the identity broker, not by policy hope.

Only after approval does a scoped, delegated authority post to the GL or release the payment. This gate protects an irreversible action, which is the strongest contrast with the Developer vertical's revertible draft PR.

view.caption: The agent (maker) proposes; a separate checker authorizes; the broker enforces maker != checker before any idempotent post.

steps.decisionPrompt: How do you enforce that the agent that proposed an entry cannot also approve it?
patterns: Delegation not impersonation (OAuth token exchange, act claim)

concepts.definition: The party that initiates a transaction (maker) must be distinct from the party that approves it (checker).
concepts.whyItMatters: It is the core financial control against error and fraud; for agents it must be enforced by identity, not convention.
concepts.example: The broker rejects an approval whose checker principal equals the proposal's maker.

whyNow: The gate is what makes propose-only autonomy safe for irreversible money movement.

recap.before: A proposal could be self-approved or posted unreviewed.
recap.after: A distinct checker authorizes; segregation of duties is enforced by identity.
recap.newRisk: Approval triggers a real post/payment — a crash or retry must not double-apply it.

traps.trap: Enforcing segregation of duties with a policy doc or a UI rule.
traps.why: Agents and retries route around UI rules; the control must be in the authorization path.
traps.instead: Have the identity broker reject any approval where checker == maker.

steps.title: 5. Exactly-Once: Idempotent Posting & Payment
description: Approval authorizes an irreversible action, so the post and the payment must be exactly-once. Every posting and payment carries an idempotency key; the GL and the payment rail dedupe on it, so a retry after a timeout or crash never double-posts or double-pays.
This is the Foundations durable-execution discipline applied where it matters most: the side effect is money, so 'resume' must skip already-committed actions.

view.caption: Approved posts and payments carry an idempotency key; the GL and rail dedupe so a retry never double-applies.

steps.decisionPrompt: The post succeeds but the response is lost and the pipeline retries — what prevents a double posting?

concepts.definition: A post/payment keyed so that repeating it has no additional effect beyond the first success.
concepts.whyItMatters: Money movement under retries is the canonical place double-execution causes real loss.
concepts.example: The GL records 'idempotency_key=K posted'; a retry with K is a no-op returning the same journalId.

whyNow: Idempotency must wrap the gate's irreversible action before the system can be trusted to retry safely.

recap.before: A retry could double-post or double-pay.
recap.after: Posts and payments are exactly-once via idempotency keys.
recap.newRisk: Auditors and regulators need to see WHY each entry was made — and not from raw model reasoning.

steps.title: 6. The Audit Artifact: An Immutable Decision Record
description: Finance must be a glass box. For every action the platform writes an immutable decision record — not a chain-of-thought dump. It captures the auditable evidence and decision: retrieved records and their source versions, tool calls, deterministic calculations, policy/control checks, maker and checker approvals, model-output summaries, exception reasons, and immutable event IDs.

Raw prompts and model reasoning are kept behind stricter retention, redaction, and access controls: hidden reasoning is not a stable audit log and can leak secrets or carry prompt-injection artifacts. This record is what continuous SOX/ICFR controls and external auditors rely on.

view.caption: Every step appends to an immutable decision record (evidence, calculations, controls, approvals, event IDs) — not raw reasoning.

steps.decisionPrompt: Why is logging the model's reasoning chain the wrong audit artifact?

concepts.definition: An audit-grade record of evidence, calculations, controls, and approvals — not the model's raw chain-of-thought.
concepts.whyItMatters: Reasoning is unstable and can leak sensitive data or injection artifacts; auditors need verifiable evidence, not a transcript.
concepts.example: The record shows 'invoice v3 matched PO #123, three-way pass, checker Jane approved at T' with event IDs.

whyNow: Auditability is a hard requirement; the record is the artifact every control and regulator depends on.

deepDives.title: Continuous SOX/ICFR controls
points: Segregation of duties, thresholds, and approvals are logged as control evidence, not just enforced.
The decision record is append-only and immutable, so it can't be edited after the fact.
Keep raw model traces in a separate, access-controlled store with shorter retention.

recap.before: The 'why' lived in opaque model output.
recap.after: Every action has an immutable, auditable decision record.
recap.newRisk: We still need to decide what posts automatically vs escalates, and to catch fraud and errors.

steps.title: 7. Thresholds, Exceptions, Fraud & Evaluation
description: Make autonomy safe and measurable. Deterministic thresholds decide what a checker must approve vs what low-risk, well-matched items may auto-execute under control — and on any ambiguity the platform halts and escalates rather than guessing.

Controls include fraud checks such as vendor bank-account-change detection (a business-email-compromise vector). And the pipeline is evaluated against ground-truth bookings: track extraction/coding accuracy and exception rates, and use trajectory eval so a high auto-post rate never hides systematic miscoding.

view.caption: Deterministic thresholds route auto-execute vs escalate; fraud controls run; the pipeline is evaluated against ground-truth bookings.

steps.decisionPrompt: Where do you set the threshold between auto-posting and routing to a checker, and what happens on ambiguity?

concepts.definition: A deterministic rule (amount, match confidence, vendor risk) that decides whether an item posts automatically or needs a checker.
concepts.whyItMatters: It bounds autonomy by risk; on ambiguity the safe default is escalation, not a guess.
concepts.example: Under $1k, exact three-way match, known vendor -> auto; otherwise -> checker.

whyNow: Thresholds, fraud controls, and eval are what let the platform run at scale without losing correctness.

recap.before: Every item needed a human and quality was unmeasured.
recap.after: Low-risk items auto-execute under control; fraud is screened; accuracy is evaluated against ground truth.
recap.newRisk: Other verticals (legal) protect a different irreversible action — citations and filings — see the rest of the series.

finalDesign.title: Final Design — Agentic Finance Platform
finalDesign.description: Source documents enter through the finance API and queue into a deterministic pipeline. The pipeline extracts and codes each transaction in a bounded LLM node, runs a deterministic three-way/PO match and threshold check, screens documents for injection and runs SOX/ICFR controls and fraud checks (e.g. vendor bank-change), and writes a proposed journal to a separate proposals store — never the GL. A checker distinct from the maker approves; the identity broker enforces segregation of duties and only then authorizes a scoped, idempotent post to the general ledger or release on the payment rail. Every step appends to an immutable decision record (evidence, calculations, controls, approvals, event IDs), with raw model traces kept separately under stricter controls. Identity/delegation, guardrails, and durable execution are the shared substrate from Agentic Platform Foundations; the priority, unlike the Developer vertical, is correctness and auditability over availability.

functional.how: Email/portal/EDI/PDF intake queued to the pipeline.
Bounded extract node plus a deterministic match engine.
Agents write proposed journals to a separate store.
Distinct checker; SoD enforced by the identity broker.
Append-only evidence/controls/approvals, not a reasoning dump.

nonFunctional.how: Halt-and-escalate on ambiguity; glass-box decision record.
Agents propose; only an approved idempotent post mutates the GL.
Idempotency keys dedupe posts and payments.
An authored pipeline with LLM in bounded nodes, not a loop.

say: Frame the inversion vs Developer: correctness and auditability over availability, because the action is irreversible.
It builds on Agentic Platform Foundations (identity, guardrails, durable execution).
Draw the agent posting directly and explain why high accuracy doesn't make it acceptable.
Ledger as ground truth (propose, don't mutate), then a deterministic pipeline with LLM in bounded nodes.
Maker-checker with SoD enforced by identity, then idempotent exactly-once posting, then the immutable decision record.
Auto-execute vs escalate thresholds, fraud (vendor bank-change), and eval against ground-truth bookings.

expectations: Agent extracts and proposes an entry.
Knows a human approves before posting.
Propose-only against a ground-truth ledger; deterministic pipeline.
Enforces segregation of duties in the authorization path, not by policy.
Designs the immutable decision record and argues against logging raw reasoning.
Picks deterministic control flow via the selection rule and ties it to SOX/ICFR; builds on the Foundations substrate.

followUps: How do you reconcile thousands of bank transactions at month-end close within the deterministic pipeline?
What exactly goes in the decision record vs the access-controlled raw-trace store, and why?
How does the identity broker prevent an agent from acting as both maker and checker across retries?
How does this irreversible-action gate differ from the legal vertical's citation/filing gate?
